name: Steak in Rye
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a large pan.
2. Wipe each steak with a damp cloth and then dry it. Coat the steak evenly with flour.
3. Over high heat, fry the steaks on both sides until they reach your desired level of doneness for rare steak.
4. Remove the steaks from the pan and keep them warm.
5. Reduce the heat to medium. Mince the green onions and sauté until golden brown.
6. Add the rye whiskey, beef extract, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Stir until the mixture is reduced by about half.
7. Place the steaks back into the pan for one minute to reheat them, turning once.
8. Transfer the steaks to a serving platter and pour the sauce over top.
Per Serving: 772.2 Cal; 70.9g Protein; 11.2g Carb; 394.4mg Sodium; 1.6g Fiber; 39.6g Fat; 20.7g SatFat; 264.7mg Chol; 361.9 Fat Cal (46.9%)
From TRUE magazine December 1964
